sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 30-year-old male, presents with right knee pain that began a week ago after an injury at a trampoline park.
He reports hearing a snap and feeling instant pain when landing.
He has not tried any treatments other than Tylenol, which provided minimal relief.
He has a history of hypertension and diabetes.
Past medical history: The patient has hypertension and diabetes, for which he is taking lisinopril 20mg and metformin 500mg, respectively.
He has not been taking his medications consistently and has not measured his blood pressure regularly.
Review of systems: The patient reports no other symptoms.
Current medications:
Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 180/80 mmHg.
Physical exam: The patient has a slight gait and a limp when walking.
The knee has ecchymosis and edema along the lateral aspect, but no effusion.
Range of motion is normal, but movement causes pain.
The heart has a regular rate and rhythm, and no murmur is appreciated.
The lungs are clear bilaterally.
Test Results: An X-ray of the right knee showed no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a lateral ligament strain in the right knee, hypertension, and diabetes.
Problem list: Lateral ligament strain of the right knee; Hypertension; Diabetes; Suspected medication non-adherence.
Plan:
I prescribed Meloxicam 15mg daily for pain and swelling in the right knee.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent further injury.
I will continue the lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension and order an echo to assess cardiac function.
I also ordered an A1C and lipid panel to evaluate the patient's diabetes management.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a follow-up appointment to discuss the results of the echo, A1C, and lipid panel, as well as to reassess the knee injury.
